claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for. Let me start with a few questions.

**First, could you tell me: How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my daughter Alex, she's 5 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! That's helpful. So you have one daughter, Alex, who is 5 years old.

Now let me ask: **Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's good to know.

**Next question: Are you the only person claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is someone else (such as a partner or ex-partner) also claiming or planning to claim for her?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm the only one - nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. That's useful to know.

**One more question: Does Alex attend school or any form of approved education or training currently?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, she's 5, so she's just started primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That makes sense at her age.

**Just to confirm a couple of final details: Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or any other right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:
- You have one daughter, Alex, who is 5 years old
- You live in the UK and Alex lives with you
- You are responsible for her care
- You are a UK citizen
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

You meet all the key requirements for Child Benefit eligibility. You are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you, you live in the UK, and you have the right to reside here as a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex!**

You should now proceed to make a claim if you haven't already done so. If you have any other questions about your circumstances or need further support with your claim, feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which means she is under 16. The claimant lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex (she lives with them at home), and is a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK. Only one person is claiming for Alex. All the core eligibility criteria are met: responsibility for a child under 16, living in the UK, and right to reside.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK, they are a UK citizen, and there are no other claimants for the same child.">
